network.overall_strategy: The network is anchored in literature-supported prebiotic routes that converge on proline precursors rather than inventing unsupported chemistry. It integrates: (i) hydrothermal protometabolic generation of alpha-ketoglutarate and glutamate on FeS/pyrite/greigite and Ni catalysts; (ii) the glutamate -> glutamic-gamma-semialdehyde -> Delta1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate -> proline branch, with spontaneous ring closure at the GSA stage; (iii) the Patel et al. cyanosulfidic route via a 4-carbon cyanohydrin, cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide, gamma-butyrothiolactam, and proline aminonitrile under UV/Cu photoredox conditions; (iv) HCN oligomerization/polymer hydrolysis as a distinct surface source of racemic proline; and (v) direct spark-discharge production of proline and glutamate as atmospheric/surface inputs. Because the literature does not demonstrate an unambiguous fully prebiotic aqueous route from racemic proline to enantiopure L-proline, the final step is represented as a selective enrichment node based on crystal-solution eutectic amplification of proline, explicitly marked as speculative in prebiotic relevance. Hub molecules are HCN, alpha-ketoglutarate, glutamate, Delta1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate, and proline aminonitrile, which provide convergence across hydrothermal, surface, and biochemical settings.

reactions.name: Hydrothermal protometabolic formation of glyoxylate and pyruvate from CO2

conditions.other: anoxic alkaline vent conditions with H2 from serpentinization

reactions.mechanism: Iron-sulfur and iron-oxide mediated CO2 reduction and C-C bond-forming protometabolic chemistry generate small alpha-keto acid precursors including glyoxylate and pyruvate.
reactions.reasoning: This reaction compresses literature-supported hydrothermal protometabolic carbon fixation to the specific intermediates needed downstream, while avoiding unsupported direct one-step claims to proline. FeS/pyrite/greigite systems and protometabolic keto-acid production are supported by Russell & Hall (1997, 2006), Muchowska et al. (2017), Stubbs et al. (2020), and Springsteen et al. (2018). Greigite and magnetite are included because hydrothermal CO2 reduction on Fe-S/Fe-oxide minerals is central to the vent framework.

reactions.name: Hydrothermal protometabolic generation of alpha-ketoglutarate

conditions.other: anoxic hydrothermal setting; reductive/oxidative protometabolic network analog

reactions.mechanism: Alpha-keto acid network chemistry couples glyoxylate and pyruvate-derived carbon flux to alpha-ketoglutarate as a branch-point TCA analog intermediate.
reactions.reasoning: Alpha-ketoglutarate is identified in the literature as the central branch-point for proline chemistry. Pyrite-driven and alpha-keto-acid network chemistry producing TCA-like intermediates including alpha-ketoglutarate are supported by Muchowska et al. (2017), Springsteen et al. (2018), and Stubbs et al. (2020). This representation is grounded in the reported network behavior rather than an unsupported single elementary step.

reactions.name: Nickel-catalyzed reductive amination of alpha-ketoglutarate to glutamate

conditions.catalyst: Ni/SiO2-Al2O3 or meteoritic Ni with Fe-rich hydrothermal minerals present
conditions.other: pH 8, anoxic mild aqueous system linked to hydrothermal H2

reactions.mechanism: Catalytic reductive amination converts the alpha-keto acid to the corresponding amino acid, yielding glutamate from alpha-ketoglutarate.
reactions.reasoning: Kaur, Moran et al. (2024) directly demonstrated reductive amination of keto acids to amino acids including glutamate using H2 and NH3 over nickel catalysts at 22C, pH 8, 1-10 bar H2. Huber & Wachtershauser (1998, 2003) further support prebiotic reductive amination on metal sulfides. Fe-rich mineral agents are included to satisfy the hydrothermal mineral requirement and reflect plausible co-occurring Fe-S vent phases, but the key literature catalyst is Ni as reported by Kaur et al. (2024).

reactions.name: Selective reduction of glutamate gamma-carboxyl to glutamic-gamma-semialdehyde

conditions.other: alkaline hydrothermal vent conditions; reductive setting

reactions.mechanism: Partial reduction of the gamma-carboxyl group of glutamate forms the corresponding semialdehyde, preserving the alpha-amino acid framework.
reactions.reasoning: The research report identifies glutamate -> GSA as the biologically analogous and prebiotically plausible but experimentally underconstrained bottleneck. The use of FeS/H2S or Fe2+ as reductants is explicitly noted as plausible in the report, while Huber & Wachtershauser (1998, 2003) support sulfide-mineral mediated reductive amino acid chemistry. This step is therefore retained as necessary but clearly acknowledged as the main speculative connection within an otherwise literature-grounded route.

reactions.name: Spontaneous cyclization of glutamic-gamma-semialdehyde to Delta1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate

conditions.other: non-enzymatic intramolecular imine formation in aqueous solution

reactions.mechanism: The semialdehyde undergoes spontaneous intramolecular condensation to a cyclic imine/carboxylate, generating the pyrroline ring system.
reactions.reasoning: This is one of the strongest steps in the entire network: Vogel & Davis (1952) established spontaneous non-enzymatic cyclization of GSA to P5C in aqueous solution, and the report emphasizes that no catalyst is required. Strecker (1957) further contextualized the biological glutamate/proline pathway.

reactions.name: Reduction of Delta1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate to proline
reactions.mechanism: Reduction of the cyclic imine/carboxylate furnishes the saturated pyrrolidine amino acid proline.
reactions.reasoning: The report explicitly states that final reduction of P5C to proline could be driven by Fe2+, FeS/H2S systems, or molecular hydrogen from serpentinization. This makes the hydrothermal reducing step plausible though not directly demonstrated as a dedicated prebiotic proline synthesis experiment. The rationale is consistent with Kelley et al. (2001) for H2-rich vent settings and Huber & Wachtershauser (1998, 2003) for metal-sulfide mediated amino-acid-forming reductions.

reactions.name: Cyanosulfidic generation of 4-carbon cyanohydrin precursor

conditions.catalyst: Cu(I)/Cu(II) photoredox cycling with phosphate-buffered anoxic solution
conditions.other: neutral pH, UV irradiation, 35C, cyanosulfidic chemistry

reactions.mechanism: HCN-derived carbonyl/cyanohydrin chemistry under sulfide-assisted UV photoredox conditions generates the 4-carbon cyanohydrin branch entering proline movement c.
reactions.reasoning: Patel et al. (2015) reported the cyanosulfidic network under 35C, neutral pH, anoxic UV-irradiated conditions with Cu photoredox cycling, producing amino-acid precursors including the specific movement leading to proline. This node captures the reported 4-carbon cyanohydrin entry without inventing alternative feedstocks.

reactions.name: Conversion of 4-carbon cyanohydrin to cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide

conditions.catalyst: UV-driven Cu(I)/Cu(II) photoredox with sulfide donor
conditions.other: neutral pH, anoxic, shallow UV-exposed pool

reactions.mechanism: H2S addition to the 4-carbon cyanohydrin generates open-chain and cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide species, with the cyclic form entering the ring-forming branch.
reactions.reasoning: Patel et al. (2015) explicitly describe proline formation through reaction of a 4-carbon cyanohydrin with H2S to form open-chain and cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamides. The surface setting with UV and sulfide is directly literature-grounded.

reactions.name: UV-driven cyclization and deoxygenation to gamma-butyrothiolactam

conditions.catalyst: UV irradiation under cyanosulfidic photoredox conditions

reactions.mechanism: Photochemical cyclization and deoxygenation of the hydroxythioamide furnish the cyclic thiolactam gamma-butyrothiolactam.
reactions.reasoning: Patel et al. (2015) specifically identify gamma-butyrothiolactam as a key intermediate in the proline branch of the cyanosulfidic network. Including TiO2 as a surface mineral agent is consistent with UV-driven photocatalytic shallow-pool chemistry and Saladino et al. (2017), while the mechanistic identity follows Patel et al. (2015).

reactions.name: Photoreduction and cyanide addition to form proline aminonitrile
reactions.mechanism: Gamma-butyrothiolactam undergoes photoreduction and HCN addition to produce the alpha-aminonitrile precursor to proline.
reactions.reasoning: Patel et al. (2015) report that gamma-butyrothiolactam gives the alpha-aminonitrile precursor of proline (compound 47) after photoreduction and HCN addition. This is the best-supported dedicated prebiotic route to a direct proline precursor.

reactions.name: Hydrolysis of proline aminonitrile to proline

conditions.other: prebiotic pool hydrolysis after cyanosulfidic transport/concentration

reactions.mechanism: Aminonitrile hydrolysis yields the corresponding amino acid proline.
reactions.reasoning: Patel et al. (2015) identify the proline alpha-aminonitrile as the immediate precursor whose hydrolysis gives proline. Aminonitrile-to-amino-acid hydrolysis is also consistent with classic Strecker-type prebiotic chemistry.

reactions.name: HCN tetramerization to DAMN

conditions.other: anoxic HCN-rich evaporitic or shallow pool conditions

reactions.mechanism: HCN oligomerizes to the tetramer diaminomaleonitrile as a standard entry into HCN polymer chemistry.
reactions.reasoning: HCN oligomerization through DAMN originates with Oro (1961) and is reviewed as a key precursor manifold by Ruiz-Bermejo et al. (2013). This is a literature-supported gateway into polymer/hydantoin pathways that can yield proline after hydrolysis.

reactions.name: HCN polymer/hydantoin progression toward proline precursor
reactions.mechanism: HCN polymer chemistry progresses through hydantoin-like intermediates that release amino acids upon hydrolysis.
reactions.reasoning: The report states that HCN oligomerization to amino acids proceeds through DAMN and hydantoin intermediates, and that proline is detected in some HCN polymer hydrolysis experiments, especially at higher temperatures. This node avoids the criticized over-compression of a direct DAMN -> proline jump and follows Ruiz-Bermejo et al. (2013).

reactions.name: Hydrolysis of hydantoin intermediate to proline

conditions.catalyst: thermal aqueous hydrolysis with surface concentration
conditions.other: anoxic higher-temperature hydrolysis of HCN-derived polymer material

reactions.mechanism: Hydrolytic opening of HCN-derived hydantoin intermediates releases amino acids including proline.
reactions.reasoning: Ruiz-Bermejo et al. (2013) review experimental HCN polymer hydrolysis conditions yielding amino acids including proline, with hydantoins implicated as intermediates. This is retained as a literature-backed but mixture-generating source of racemic proline.

reactions.name: Spark-discharge atmospheric synthesis of HCN and amino-acid feedstocks

conditions.temperature: variable discharge plasma with condensed aqueous collection
conditions.other: reducing atmospheric mixture with electrical discharge and post-discharge aqueous trapping

reactions.mechanism: Electric discharge through reducing gas mixtures generates nitriles, carbonyls, HCN, and amino acids including glutamate and proline, which are trapped in aqueous condensates.
reactions.reasoning: Ring et al. (1972) definitively identified proline in Miller-Urey spark discharge products; Johnson et al. (2008) and Jiang et al. (2014) expanded amino acid detection in archived or related apparatus. The failed prior attempt was criticized for using NH3 alone; here the full CH4/N2/H2O/NH3 discharge input is specified in line with the literature. Mineral agents are included as plausible concentration surfaces for the required surface-reaction schema, though the synthesis driver is electrical discharge.

reactions.name: Routing of spark-derived glutamate into the glutamate-proline branch

conditions.other: transport of surface-derived glutamate into vent-associated reducing settings

reactions.mechanism: Surface-generated glutamate is imported into a hydrothermal FeS/H2 system where selective gamma-reduction furnishes GSA.
reactions.reasoning: This cross-environment connector is necessary to make the graph connected and is consistent with the report's emphasis that products from surface environments can feed hydrothermal/protometabolic pathways. The specific chemistry inherits the same literature basis and uncertainty as glutamate -> GSA in the main branch: plausible via FeS/H2S or Fe2+ reductive conditions noted in the report, with general sulfide-mineral support from Huber & Wachtershauser (1998, 2003).

reactions.name: Selective enrichment of L-proline from racemic proline by eutectic phase behavior

conditions.other: crystal-solution eutectic amplification; demonstrated in CHCl3/EtOH, prebiotic relevance unresolved

reactions.mechanism: Differential solubility of racemic and enantiopure solid phases enriches the dissolved proline pool in the L-enantiomer under eutectic conditions.
reactions.reasoning: Hayashi et al. (2006) showed dramatic ee amplification for proline by crystal-solution eutectic behavior, and Klussmann et al. (2006) quantified proline eutectic properties. However, the research report emphasizes that the demonstration used non-prebiotic organic solvents and has not been translated to aqueous prebiotic conditions. This step is therefore explicitly speculative as a route to the named target L-proline, but it is the closest literature-grounded enrichment mechanism available.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal alpha-ketoglutarate to proline core route
pathways.description: CO2/H2 hydrothermal protometabolism yields alpha-ketoglutarate, which is reductively aminated to glutamate and carried through GSA and P5C to proline.

pathways.name: Cyanosulfidic movement c route
pathways.description: HCN/H2S/UV/Cu chemistry in shallow anoxic pools proceeds through a 4-carbon cyanohydrin, cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide, gamma-butyrothiolactam, and proline aminonitrile to proline.

pathways.name: HCN oligomerization and hydantoin hydrolysis route
pathways.description: HCN oligomerizes to DAMN, progresses through HCN polymer/hydantoin intermediates, and hydrolyzes to proline.

pathways.name: Direct spark-discharge proline route
pathways.description: Electric discharge through reducing gases directly generates racemic proline, followed by selective enrichment toward L-proline.

pathways.name: Spark-discharge glutamate to hydrothermal proline route
pathways.description: Spark-discharge chemistry supplies glutamate, which is transported into FeS/H2 hydrothermal settings and converted through GSA and P5C to proline.

pathways.name: Spark-generated HCN feeding cyanosulfidic proline synthesis
pathways.description: HCN produced in atmospheric discharge is funneled into the Patel-Sutherland surface network to make proline aminonitrile and then proline.

pathways.name: Spark-generated HCN feeding HCN polymer hydrolysis route
pathways.description: Atmospheric discharge supplies HCN, which oligomerizes to DAMN and proceeds through hydantoin chemistry to proline.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal alpha-ketoglutarate branch with biochemical ring closure emphasis
pathways.description: This pathway highlights alpha-ketoglutarate as the metabolic branch point and P5C as the spontaneous ring-forming convergence node before reduction to proline.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal precursor generation converging on the glutamate branch
pathways.description: CO2 reduction generates glyoxylate and pyruvate, which converge to alpha-ketoglutarate and then flow through glutamate, GSA, and P5C to proline.

pathways.name: Dual-surface to biochemical convergence on proline
pathways.description: Independent surface routes—cyanosulfidic aminonitrile chemistry and HCN polymer hydrolysis—both converge on racemic proline, which then enters a common enrichment step.
